page: Vultr vc2-24c-96gb is a Cloud Compute instance featuring 24 vCPUs and 96.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio. Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.0 GHz, the system provides 24 physical cores with one thread per core under a shared CPU allocation model. The processor architecture includes 32 KB L1d cache, 4 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache, alongside support for AVX, AVX512F, AES, and FMA instruction sets. Local storage is bundled with 1600 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results place this instance consistently in the average tier across most performance metrics. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance registers at 1451.3, while multi-core performance reaches 32078.94. Memory bandwidth tests show 2192.16 GB/sec for small block cached reads, dropping to 114.95 GB/sec for large block uncached reads and 55.93 GB/sec for large block writes. Single-threaded gzip compression is a relative bottleneck at 23.37 GB/sec, falling into the weak tier, though multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performs adequately at 98.25 GB/sec. Database and web serving capabilities are supported by a Redis SET throughput of 31.68 million operations per second and a static web serving throughput of 15.77 GB/sec.

Given its shared CPU allocation, the instance offers a cost-efficient path to high core counts and substantial memory density without the premium of dedicated hardware. However, the shared CPU model introduces potential performance variability under sustained heavy loads. This hardware profile is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, medium-sized database operations, static web serving, and lightweight machine learning tasks, as demonstrated by LLM inference speeds of 33.17 tokens per second for prompt processing on a 7B model.
description: Vultr vc2-24c-96gb is a Cloud Compute instance configured with 24 vCPUs, 96.0 GB of RAM, and 1600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.0 GHz, this server operates on a shared CPU allocation model. Performance benchmarks generally fall within the average range, featuring a Passmark CPU score of 29089.76 and a Redis SET throughput of 31.68 million operations per second. While single-threaded gzip compression is relatively weak at 23.37 GB/sec, the instance delivers solid multi-threaded capabilities, as shown by a bzip3 decompression speed of 98.25 GB/sec. This resource configuration provides a cost-effective option for workloads requiring high memory capacity and multi-core processing, such as database operations, static web serving, and moderate multi-threaded application hosting, though users should consider the potential variability of shared CPU resources.
og_description: Vultr vc2-24c-96gb is a Cloud Compute server featuring 24 vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 1600 GB NVMe SSD storage. It utilizes a shared Intel Xeon processor to deliver balanced, cost-effective multi-core performance.
meta_description: Vultr vc2-24c-96gb is a Cloud Compute server with 24 vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 1600 GB NVMe storage, offering balanced shared-CPU performance.
tagline: A balanced multi-core virtual server combining substantial memory and fast NVMe storage for diverse, non-dedicated computing workloads.
bullet_points: 24 Intel Xeon vCPUs on a shared CPU allocation model
96.0 GB of system memory paired with 1600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Average multi-core performance with a Passmark CPU score of 29089.76
Redis throughput of 31.68 million operations per second for caching workloads
Suited for static web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded applications
